title: Parvovirus B19
body_html:
Parvovirus B19 is a DNA virus which causes a variety of clinical presentations. It was identified in the 1980's as the cause of erythema infectiosum

Erythema infectiosum (also known as fifth disease or 'slapped-cheek syndrome')
  • most common presenting illness
  • systemic symptoms: lethargy, fever, headache
  • 'slapped-cheek' rash spreading to proximal arms and extensor surfaces

Other presentations
  • asymptomatic
  • pancytopaenia in immunosuppressed patients
  • aplastic crises e.g. in sickle-cell disease (parvovirus B19 suppresses erythropoiesis for about a week so aplastic anaemia is rare unless there is a chronic haemolytic anaemia)
